claimText: Alternative Investment Funds (AIFs) are privately pooled investment vehicles regulated by the Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI).
evidenceExcerpt: AIFs are privately pooled investment vehicles regulated by SEBI under the 2012 regulations.
examUse: Statement-based question identifying the regulatory body for AIFs.

claimText: Category I AIFs invest in start-ups, early-stage ventures, SMEs, and infrastructure funds.
evidenceExcerpt: Category I AIFs invest in start-ups, early-stage ventures, SMEs, and infrastructure funds.
examUse: Correct-pair question matching AIF categories with their investment focus.

claimText: Category II AIFs do not use leverage and include Private Equity Funds, Debt Funds, and Real Estate Funds.
evidenceExcerpt: Category II AIFs do not use leverage and include Private Equity Funds, Debt Funds, and Real Estate Funds.
examUse: Statement-based question on the characteristics and examples of Category II AIFs.

claimText: Category III AIFs may use leverage and include Hedge Funds and Private investment in public equity (PIPE).
evidenceExcerpt: Category III AIFs may use leverage and include Hedge Funds and Private investment in public equity (PIPE).
examUse: Statement-based question on which AIF category is permitted to use leverage.

claimText: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) exempted the government-backed SWAMIH Fund from tightened Alternative Investment Fund (AIF) rules.
evidenceExcerpt: RBI exempted the government-backed SWAMIH Fund from tightened AIF rules.
examUse: Statement-based question on regulatory exemptions for specific government-backed funds.
